preserved_inputs.R8D: entry type/mode dword copied from the caller
preserved_inputs.R9: caller-supplied object later reused as the left half of the returned pair

factory_structure.role: Global deduplicating factory that scans a registry for an existing entry keyed by label text plus mode bits, returns the existing pair on match, and otherwise allocates a fresh 0xA10-byte entry object.
factory_structure.evidence_source: Fresh headless no-analysis dump in this pass on rs2quick26.

evidence.observation: loads the global list head from 0x140C39D28 and iterates until the sentinel is reached, proving the helper walks a shared registry rather than operating only on caller-local state.
acquires a temporary reference on candidate [RBX +0x10], showing each registry node owns a refcounted side object used during matching.
normalizes the incoming label string object at R15 and the candidate entry string rooted at [R14 +0x8], then compares them through 0x14010E3B0.
requires both candidate dword [R14 +0x268] == R13D and candidate byte [R14 +0x28A] == stack byte [RSP +0xA0] in addition to the string match.
on match, returns the pair {RDI, R14} through [R12] and [R12 +0x8], proving the helper is a deduplicating pair factory rather than a pure boolean lookup.
on miss, allocates a fresh 0xA10-byte object and branches into the constructor path.
calls 0x14067C440 on the new object's +0x20 region with the original label string, mode dword, and selector byte, establishing 0x14067C440 as the entry initializer.

settings_roots.statement: The earlier alternate fixed roots used by 0x140679500 are now concretely resolved as 'GlobalSettings' versus 'Settings' string roots, not opaque descriptor blobs.

publisher_anchor.role: Side-object publisher that consumes the record built by 0x140679500 and emits it through the record's +0x48 object.

evidence.observation: loads RBX = [RCX +0x48], proving the helper's primary sink is the refcounted side object previously installed at +0x48 by 0x140679500.

seeds two temporary descriptor objects from 0x140BA0158 and 0x140BA0188; nearby helper thunks 0x14067B2B0 and 0x14067B2F0 show those roots are descriptor builders that pair a fixed tag with [RCX +0x8], not plain inline strings.
copies the record's label string from [RCX +0x8] into a temporary short-string object.
calls 0x1407A1270 with literal 0x140B9CD84 = '*' and a temporary output buffer, showing the publish path constructs a wildcard-related derived value from the copied label.
calls 0x14067BF80 with RCX = [record +0x48], RDX = derived wildcard-related object, R8 = descriptor pair rooted at 0x140BA0188, and R9 = descriptor pair rooted at 0x140BA0158.
releases the temporary wildcard object and both descriptor objects after the 0x14067BF80 call, proving they are transient publish-time materials.

bounded_inference.statement: 0x140679A30 is better described as a settings-entry publisher than a generic finalizer. It turns the record's copied label plus mode descriptors into temporary publish inputs and emits them through the record's +0x48 sink object.

supporting_thunks.0x14067B2B0: descriptor thunk that writes fixed root 0x140BA0158 plus payload [RCX +0x8] into the caller's output pair
supporting_thunks.0x14067B2F0: descriptor thunk that writes fixed root 0x140BA0188 plus payload [RCX +0x8] into the caller's output pair
supporting_thunks.0x14067B2E0: tail-jumps into 0x140679B70 after shifting RCX by +0x8, tying the nearby descriptor-thunk island into the publisher family

updated_model.statement: The earlier 0x19E90-driven 'totp'/'login' helper path now resolves into a concrete pipeline: 0x140679500 builds a labeled record in the Settings/GlobalSettings family, 0x14067C1F0 deduplicates or allocates the associated side entry object keyed by label and mode, and 0x140679A30 publishes that record through the side object's +0x48 sink using descriptor thunks and a wildcard-derived temporary object.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-label-helper-and-shared-template-p: Replaces the earlier generic 'refcounted side objects' wording with a concrete Settings/GlobalSettings entry-factory and publisher model.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-client-19e90-totp-login-label-cons: Explains the downstream meaning of the two earlier 0x140679500 calls on the 'totp' and 'login' labels: they are building and publishing settings-family entries rather than only formatting strings.

remaining_uncertainties: The exact semantic names of descriptor roots 0x140BA0158 and 0x140BA0188 remain unresolved, although the nearby thunks prove they are structured descriptor tags rather than raw strings.
0x14067BF80 still needs its own deeper classification to determine whether the +0x48 sink is a settings tree, serializer, DOM-like emitter, or another structured publish target.
next_targets: Classify 0x14067BF80 and the +0x48 sink object so the publication target can be named precisely.
Resolve descriptor roots 0x140BA0158 and 0x140BA0188 beyond 'descriptor tags' by following the 0x14067B2B0/0x14067B2F0 thunk family further.
Return later to the 0x1400255B6 reset window once a stable exact-address recovery path exists for fresh imports.
